title: Phanera uleana
intro: Phanera uleana, also known as the "Uleana's rattan tree," is a species of rattan found in Southeast Asia, characterized by its climbing habit and woody stem. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating various infections. The bioactive constituents of Phanera uleana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phanera uleana is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
